page: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 32 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor clocked at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks and PassMark memory scores also rank in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng falls into the average category, which reflects the shared CPU allocation model. However, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput remain strong, placing in the top 25% of tested systems.

The AMD EPYC-Turin processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration. These instructions contribute to top-tier performance in small-to-medium LLM inference text generation and strong performance in large-model text generation. Given the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and shared CPU architecture, this instance is suited for database hosting, web serving, and memory-intensive workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources.
description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD. While the shared CPU allocation places multi-core performance in the average tier, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, memory write bandwidth, and single-threaded compression speeds. Advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration enable strong performance in database operations, Redis SET transactions, and LLM inference. This instance provides a cost-effective, resource-dense profile for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-32c-128g-1920s is a general-purpose server with 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering top-tier single-core and memory performance.

tagline: A resource-dense virtual server combining 32 shared AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and high-speed NVMe storage for database and web workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 1920 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory write performance
AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction set support
Well-suited for database operations, web serving, and LLM inference
